Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 044 [Sakikku 16][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.DU-su GU₇-šu₂ U₄.DA.TAB.BA ŠU DINGIR AD-A.NI DIN GAM

(o 1) (If) he is ill for 1 day and his head hurts him: heat stroke; hand of the god of his father; he will recover (or) he will die.

o 22

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GAR.GAR ši-gu-u₂ is-si ŠU-MIN-šu₂ NIRaṣ GAM

(o 2) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he continually puts his hands on his belly, cries out in pain (and) continually stretches out his hands: he will die.

o 33

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GAR.GAR U-MEŠ-šu₂ u₂-na-ṣab ŠU DINGIR rab-bu-ti GAM

(o 3) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he continually puts his hands on his belly (and) sucks his fingers: hand of the great gods; he will die.

o 44

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ana E₂-šu₂ ER₂ : * KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ana DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ ER₂ GAM

(o 4) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he weeps for his house. : (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he weeps for his children: he will die.

o 55

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ŠE₁₀ pa₂-ru-ti-šu₂ iz-zi₂ GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ina KAŠ₃-šu₂ UŠ₂ iš-tin ana KI GIG GAM

(o 5) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he ejects excrement (in) his vomit: he will die. : (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he urinates blood in his urine: he is afflicted by the Underworld; he will die.

o 66

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma i-leb-bu u i-sa-aʾ GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma a-a GU₃.GU₃si-ma la₃ NAG ŠU DINGIR GAL.GAL GAM

(o 6) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he howls and cries out in pain: he will die. : (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he continually cries "ah!" and does not drink: hand of the great gods; he will die.

o 77

* KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma UŠ₂ MUD iz-zi GAM : ana GAL₅.LA₂ UŠ₂ pa-qid GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄)> 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma GIG-su DUGUD-ma ZU-šu₂ la₃ u₂-ad₂-da GAM

(o 7) (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) he defecates dark blood: he will die : (variant:) he is entrusted to the demon of death; he will die. : (If) ditto (he is ill for 1 day and) his illness becomes burdensome and he no longer recognizes his acquaintance: he will die.

o 88

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ ŠUB-šu₂-ma U₄ 1-KAM₂ LAL-šu₂ NIG₂.GU₇ dDIM₂.ME.LAGAB ŠU DINGIR GAL [...]

(o 8) (If the illness) pours over him for 1 day and overwhelms him for 1 day: food of the seizer-demon; hand of the great gods [...].

o 99

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ 1-KAM₂ BAR-šu₂ ZI DINGIR DABit

(o 9) (If the illness) afflicts him for 1 day, leaves him for 1 day: he is affected by an attack of a god.

o 1010

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ 1-KAM₂ ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂<(BAR-šu₂ U₄ LAL-šu₂)> UB-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ KUM₂-ma i-ra-ʾu-ub

(o 10) (If the illness) afflicts him for 1 day, (leaves him) for 1 day (gloss: new break); (when his fit overwhelms him (again)), his limbs hurt him, he is feverish and trembles, he sweats and (gloss: new break) (he is tired and) continually strongly requests (to drink water): hand of Marduk; he will recover.

o 1111

IR ŠUB-su-ma ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂<(ina-aḫ u A ana NAG ma)>-gal! URU₄-MEŠ ŠU dAMAR.UTU DIN

o 1212

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma U₄ 1-KAM₂ ba-liṭ GIDIM a-bi u AMA DAB-su

(o 12) (If) he is ill for 1 day and he is healthy for 1 day: the ghost of father or mother seized him.

o 1313

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ U₄ 2-KAM₂ i-re-eḫ-ḫi-šum-ma ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ DAB-su DABit GU₄

(o 13) (If the illness) flows over him for 1 day, 2 days and seizes him on the 3rd day: seizure of a ghost.

o 1414

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ DAB.DAB-su DAB-su ŠUB.ŠUB-šu₂ GAM

(o 14) (If) he is ill for 1 day (or) 2 days and his belly continually seizes him, his seizure continually strikes him: he will die.

o 1515

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ U₄ 2-KAM₂ U₄ 3-KAM₂ GIG-ma KUM₂ UḪ₂ ina SU-šu₂ DU₈-ma EGIR DU₈ KUM₂

(o 15) (If) he is ill for 1 day, 2 days (or) 3 days and then the burning fever lets up from his body but after it has let up, the burning fever does not let up from his head: he will die after 3 or 4 days.

o 1616

UḪ₂ ina SAG.DU-šu₂ la₃ DU₈ ana U₄ 3-KAM₂ ana U₄ 4-KAM₂ GAM

o 1717

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GU₇-šu₂ IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GISSU DU₃ ŠU dU₄.AL.TAR BUR₂-ma DIN

(o 17) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his right temple hurts him (and) his right eye forms a dark spot: the hand of Jupiter will be released and he will recover.

o 1818

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 GU₇-šu₂ IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 GISSU DU₃ ŠU dU₄.AL.TAR BUR₂-ma DIN

(o 18) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his left temple hurts him, his left eye forms a dark spot: the hand of Jupiter will be released and he will recover.

o 1919

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 GISSU

(o 19) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his right and left temples hurt him, his right and left eyes form a dark spot: that man, his god or the god of his town has cursed (him); they will be released and he will recover.

o 2020

ib-na-a LU₂ BI DINGIR-šu₂ u DINGIR IRI-šu₂ iz-zur BUR₂-<MEŠ>-ma DIN

o 21-2321-23

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ ŠUB-ma ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ DAB-šu₂ im-ta!-nag-ga-ag EGIR im-ta-na;gag-ga ra-ʾi-i-ba TUKUši UB-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SED;a EGIRnu KUM₂ TEŠ₂.BI i-rak-kab-šu₂ : ⸢i-re⸣-[eḫ-ḫi-šu₂ ...] ⸢ŠUB⸣-su-ma ina-aḫ DABit KURi DAB!-su

(o 21-23) (If the illness) pours over (him) for 2 days and then seizes him on the third day, when it has seized him he has repeated cramps (and) after he has repeated cramps, he is affected by shivering, his limbs hurt him, his hands and feet are cold (and) afterwards the fever altogether mounts in him : (variant:) pours over him [...] strikes him and he is tired: the seizure of the mountain seized him.

o 2424

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ ⸢DAB-su ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ i-re⸣-[eḫ-ḫi-šu₂ ... KUR]⸢i⸣ GAM

(o 24) (If the illness) afflicts him for 2 days (and) pours over him on the third day: [...] of the mountain; he will die.

o 2525

* U₄ [x]-KAM₂ [...] ⸢GIG⸣ ki-iṣ-ṣa-tu₂ GIG

(o 25) (If) [...] days [...] he has the kiṣṣatu-illness,

o 2626

[...]-⸢ma GAM⸣

(o 26) [...] and he will die.

(end obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

* ⸢U₄ 6⸣-[KAM₂ ...]

(r 1') (If) 6 days [...] he is ill and on [...].

r 2'2'

GIG-ma ina [...]

r 3'3'

* ina U₄ 15-KAM₂ [...]

(r 3') (If) on the 15th day [...].

r 4'4'

* a-di-na ITI la₃ GIG-ma iṣ-lim-ma <kir>-⸢ra-šu₂⸣ [...] ⸢GIG⸣-ma [...]

(r 4') (If) he was not ill yet for a month and then turned flushed and his collarbone [...]: he is ill and [...].

r 5'5'

* KI.MIN<(a-di-na)> ITI la₃ GIG-ma iṣ-lim-ma IGI-MIN-šu₂ rab-ṣa ⸢GIG⸣-ma [...]

(r 5') (If) ditto (he was not ill yet for a month) and then turned flushed and his eyes stare: he is ill and [...].

r 6'6'

* ITI 1-KAM₂ ITI 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma GIG-su <ŠUB>-šu₂-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ ⸢DAB⸣.DAB-su!(TU₂) ina KIR₄-šu₂ UŠ₂ pe-la-a ŠUB.⸢ŠUB⸣a GIG1

(r 6') (If) he is ill for 1 month, 2 months and then his illness pours over him and his belly continually seizes (him), red blood continually pours from his mouth: he has the (end of line r7':) kiṣṣatu-illness; he will recover.

r 7'7'

* ina ITI 1-KAM₂ ITI 2-KAM₂ GE₆ ŠUB-su u GU₃si pi-rit-tu ŠU d15 ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂: ki-iṣ-ṣa-tu₂ GIG DIN

(r 7') (If) for 1 month, 2 months, (the illness) falls on him at night and he shouts: terror of the hand of Ištar (gloss: new break).

r 8'8'

* u₄-mi-šam-ma <<ana>> GISKIM-šu₂ LA₂-šu₂ u BAR-šu₂ U₄ LA₂-šu₂ KUM₂-ma SA-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ IR ŠUB-su!-ma ina-aḫ LA₂ti U₄.DA

(r 8') (If) his sign daily overwhelms him and then leaves him, when it overwhelms him he is feverish and his muscles hurt him, he sweats, he is tired: fit of sunstroke.

r 9'9'

* u₄-mi ma-aʾ-du-ti GIG-ma A SA₅-ME ina DUR₂-šu₂ DU-ME GIG-su DU₈ U₄.DA.TAB.BA DIN U₄-MEŠ-šu₂2

(r 9') (If) he is ill for many days and a red liquid flows from his anus: his illness is released; heat stroke; he will recover; his days (end of line 10':) will be long; there is no fate.

r 10'10'

* KI.MIN<(u₄-mi)> ma-aʾ-du-ti GIG-ma SA-MEŠ-šu₂ SIG₇ ŠUBu₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ UŠ₂ SA₅-MEŠ GAM : GID₂.DA-MEŠ ši-ma-ti NU GAL₂ši

(r 10') (If) ditto (he is ill for many days) and his veins become yellow-green, his eyes become full of blood: he will die.

r 11'11'

* KI.MIN<(u₄-mi)> ma-aʾ-du-ti GIG-ma UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ ur-qa₂ it-ta-du-ni IGI-MIN-šu₂ SA₅ ŠUBni NU DIN

(r 11') (If) ditto (he is ill for many days) and his flesh becomes pale, his eyes get red spot(s): he will not recover.

r 12'12'

* U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ana E₂-šu₂ ER₂ GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ana E₂-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂ER₂ DIN GAM

(r 12') (If) his days are long and he weeps for his house: he will die. : (If) ditto (his days are long) and he weeps for his house (gloss: new break): he will live (or) he will die.

r 13'13'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma i-siḫ-ti KI.MAḪ-šu₂ i-siḫ GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ŠE₁₀ pa₂!-ru-<(ti-šu₂)> ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂<(iz)>-zi GAM

(r 13') (If) ditto (his days are long and) he made the allocation of his grave: he will die. : (If) ditto (his days are long) and he ejects excrement (in) his vomit (gloss: new break): he will die.

r 14'14'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma IGI-MIN-MEŠ-šu₂ GIM si-ka-a-ti ana GA.RAŠsar SIG₇-ME GAM

(r 14') (If) ditto (his days are long and) his eyes are yellow-green like powdered leeks: he will die.

r 15'15'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma SU-šu₂ GIM KI.MIN<(si-ka-a-ti)> ana GA.RAŠsar SIG₇ ŠA₃-MEŠ-šu₂ nap-ḫu SA ŠA₃-šu₂ SIG₇ GIB-ME GAM

(r 15') (If) ditto (his days are long and) his body is yellow-green like ditto (leek powder), his intestines are swollen, the vein(s) of his belly are yellow-green (and) lying across (it): he will die.

r 16'16'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ma-la GIG-su KUR₂-šum-ma UGU-šu₂ i-sap-pi-du-ma DIN

(r 16') (If) ditto (his days are long and), his hands and feet are full (of spots): his illness will change him; they will mourn over him but he will recover.

r 17'17'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ik-tap-pa₂ GIG-su KUR₂-šum-ma UGU-šu₂ KI.MIN<(i-sap-pi-du-ma)> DIN

(r 17') (If) ditto (his days are long and) his hands and feet have curved: his illness will change him; (they will mourn) over him (but) he will recover.

r 18'18'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ik-ra-a GIG-su KUR₂-šum-ma UGU-šu₂ KI.MIN<(i-sap-pi-du-ma)> DIN

(r 18') (If) ditto (his days are long and) his hands and feet have contracted: his illness will change him; (they will mourn) over him (but) he will recover.

r 19'19'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma UŠ₂ ina KIR₄ DUku DIN : * KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma GU₇ u NAG la₃ ZU KURUM₆ KI.MAḪ-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂

(r 19') (If) ditto (his days are long and) blood flows from his nose: he will recover. : (If) ditto (his days are long and) he (no longer) knows how to eat and drink: ration of his grave (gloss: break).

r 20'20'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ḫu-qu DAB.DAB-su gišNU.UR₂.MA URU₄iš GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma ZU₂.LUM.MA URU₄-ma GU₇ DIN

(r 20') (If) ditto (his days are long and) crisis continually seizes him (and) he requests pomegranates: he will die. : (If) ditto (his days are long and) he requests and eats dates: he will recover.

r 21'21'

* KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma DILIM₂ NINDA URU₄-ma I₃ NAG GAM : * KI.MIN<(U₄-MEŠ-šu₂)> GID₂.DA-MEŠ-ma GA URU₄-ma I₃ NAG ana KI GIG GAM

(r 21') (If) ditto (his days are long) and he requests a bowl of bread and drinks oil: he will die. : (If) ditto (his days are long) and he requests milk and drinks oil: he has a disease from the Underworld; he will die.

r 22'22'

DUB 2-KAM₂ * U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma GAR TAGti DUB 16-KAM₂ * U₄ma ana E₂ GIG KA.PIRIG DUku la₃3

(r 22') Tablet 2 of "(If) he is ill for 1 day and the nature of the affliction", tablet 16 "When an āšipu goes to a patient's house" not (end of r23':) finished.

(colophon)
r 23'23'

* ina SAG GIG-šu₂ IR bu-uḫ₂-bu!-uḫ₂!-tu₂ TUKU-ma IR ši-i TA kin₃-ṣi : AL.TIL

(r 23') "(If), at the beginning of his illness, he sweats, has boil(s) and this sweating from the shin (...)".

r 24'24'

TA gišDA LIBIR! up-pu-šu₂ SAR-ma ba-ru₃

(r 24') Executed properly, written and checked from an old writing-board.

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

IM mBAša₂-a bu₁₂-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ ŠU-MIN

(t.e. 1) Tablet of Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, the exorcist. Hands of Anu-ab-uṣur, son of Anu-mukin-apli, descendant of Kuri.

t.e. 22

md60—AD—URI₃ DUMU ša₂ md60—DU—A A mkur-i

t.e. 33

ina INIM d60 liš-lim

(t.e. 3) At the command of Anu, may (it) go well.

1the end of this sentence is written at the end of the following line

2the end of the sentence is written at the end of line 10'

3end of the sentence at the end of the following line
